claim.content: The renormalized-band calculation exhibits a quasiparticle spectral weight associated with the narrow Kondo-derived f-electron peak that decreases smoothly with increasing magnetic field B (the computed peak amplitude and derived low-energy effective-mass indicators vary continuously and only gradually across the ≈10 T scale); under the assumption that the renormalized-band treatment captures the essential field-dependent many-body physics responsible for Kondo screening in the periodic lattice, this calculated smooth decrease is taken to imply that in the real material YbRh2Si2 the Kondo-singlet-derived quasiparticle weight is suppressed by a smooth crossover as a function of B rather than by an abrupt, discontinuous (first-order) transition at that field scale.
